goal: Search and install llama.cpp for this OS architecture

assertions.description: User received information about their OS architecture
assertions.evaluation_criteria: Output identifies the current operating system (macOS/Linux/Windows) and CPU architecture (x86_64/arm64/etc.)

assertions.description: User was informed about llama.cpp installation options
assertions.evaluation_criteria: Output explains available installation methods (compilation from source, package managers, prebuilt binaries)
llama.cpp binary or executable is available on the system after workflow completion

assertions.description: User received confirmation and usage instructions
assertions.evaluation_criteria: Final output confirms successful installation and provides basic usage examples or next steps

assertions.description: Shell MCP was used to detect system information
assertions.evaluation_criteria: shell_mcp.execute_command() called with system detection commands (uname, arch, etc.)

assertions.description: Browser MCP searched for llama.cpp installation instructions
assertions.evaluation_criteria: browser_mcp.search() or browser_mcp.navigate() used to find llama.cpp documentation or GitHub

assertions.description: Installation commands were executed via Shell MCP
assertions.evaluation_criteria: shell_mcp.execute_command() called with installation commands (git clone, make, cmake, etc.)
Commands executed to verify llama.cpp is properly installed and functional
Workflow handled potential errors (missing dependencies, compilation failures) appropriately
